params.found: Whether the hash exists in the registry.

anchorHash(bytes32).details: The `contentHash` must be the keccak/SHA-256 of the original file buffer,      formatted as a bytes32 value. In the frontend, convert using:        `viem.toBytes(sha256HexString)` → then pass as bytes32. Reverts if:   - `contentHash` is bytes32(0) (zero hash — almost certainly a bug).   - The hash has already been registered (idempotency guard). Emits: {AssetRegistered}

params.contentHash: The 32-byte SHA-256 hash of the digital asset to register.
The 32-byte SHA-256 hash to look up.

returns._0: The ProvenanceRecord struct for the registered asset.

isRegistered(bytes32).details: Gas-cheaper than verifyAsset() when you only need a boolean.      Useful for on-chain composability (e.g., another contract checking      provenance before executing a transfer).

returns._0: True if the hash is registered, false otherwise.

verifyAsset(bytes32).details: This is a VIEW function — it does NOT write state and costs no gas      when called off-chain (e.g., via `viem.readContract()`).      It DOES emit an event to create an auditable verification trail.      NOTE: Events in view functions are not persisted. To log verifications,      call verifyAsset() as a non-view transaction, or handle off-chain.

params.contentHash: The 32-byte SHA-256 hash to look up.

returns.found: True if the hash exists in the registry.
returns.record: The full ProvenanceRecord (zero-valued if not found).

HashAlreadyRegistered(bytes32).notice: Thrown when attempting to anchor a hash that is already registered.

InvalidContentHash().notice: Thrown when a zero hash is passed to anchorHash().

AssetRegistered(bytes32,address,uint256).notice: Emitted when a new content hash is anchored for the first time.

AssetVerified(bytes32,bool,address).notice: Emitted on each call to verifyAsset(), whether or not it succeeds. Useful for building an off-chain audit trail of verification attempts.

anchorHash(bytes32).notice: Anchor a SHA-256 content hash on-chain, permanently recording         the caller as the originator of the content at this block timestamp.

getRecord(bytes32).notice: Returns the full provenance record for a given hash.         Reverts if the hash is not registered (use isRegistered() first         if you need a non-reverting check).

isRegistered(bytes32).notice: Quick boolean check — does this hash exist in the registry?

registerAsset(bytes32).notice: Alias for anchorHash(). Provided for semantic clarity —         "register" communicates intent at the product layer.

totalRegistered().notice: Total number of unique content hashes registered. Useful for dashboard statistics without needing to iterate the mapping.

verifyAsset(bytes32).notice: Verify whether a content hash has been registered, and return         its provenance metadata if found.

userdoc.notice: On-chain registry for digital content provenance on Monad Testnet. HOW IT WORKS:   1. When a user registers an image via POST /api/register, the backend      computes a SHA-256 hash of the raw file bytes.   2. After the Supabase record is saved, the frontend calls anchorHash()      with that hash to write an immutable, timestamped record on-chain.   3. During verification, verifyAsset() is called to confirm the hash      exists in the registry and return the original anchor metadata. DESIGN PRINCIPLES:   - Simplicity first: no access control beyond ownership of the anchor.   - Immutable: anchors cannot be modified or deleted after registration.   - Gas-efficient: uses a mapping for O(1) lookup.   - Event-driven: all writes emit events for off-chain indexing.

documentation.text:
@title ProvenanceRegistry
 @author Nexora
 @notice On-chain registry for digital content provenance on Monad Testnet.
 HOW IT WORKS:
   1. When a user registers an image via POST /api/register, the backend
      computes a SHA-256 hash of the raw file bytes.
   2. After the Supabase record is saved, the frontend calls anchorHash()
      with that hash to write an immutable, timestamped record on-chain.
   3. During verification, verifyAsset() is called to confirm the hash
      exists in the registry and return the original anchor metadata.
 DESIGN PRINCIPLES:
   - Simplicity first: no access control beyond ownership of the anchor.
   - Immutable: anchors cannot be modified or deleted after registration.
   - Gas-efficient: uses a mapping for O(1) lookup.
   - Event-driven: all writes emit events for off-chain indexing.

@notice Maps a content hash (bytes32 SHA-256) to its provenance record.
 Lookup is O(1) regardless of how many assets are registered.

@notice Total number of unique content hashes registered.
 Useful for dashboard statistics without needing to iterate the mapping.
@notice Thrown when attempting to anchor a hash that is already registered.
@notice Thrown when a zero hash is passed to anchorHash().
